title: St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala
tagline: St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: St Thomas Cathedral, also known as the Cathedral of Pala, is a Catholic church that follows the Syro-Malabar rite and is located in the town of Pala in the state of Kerala in the s
overview.description: St Thomas Cathedral, also known as the Cathedral of Pala, is a Catholic church that follows the Syro-Malabar rite and is located in the town of Pala in the state of Kerala in the south of the Asian country of India. It serves as the mother church of the Syro-Malabar Diocese or Eparchy of Palai ( Eparchia Palaiensis) which was created in 1950 through the bull "Quo Ecclesiarum" of Pope Pius XII. The church was founded in the year 1002 July 3, by four Syrian Christian families. These four Christian families of Palai were Tharayil, Koottumkal (brother of Tharayil Mappila), Erakonny and Vayalakombi

topPlaces.name: Katappattor
topPlaces.description: Kadappattoor is a small village on the banks of Meenachil river near Pala in Kottayam district of Kerala, 30 km east of Kottayam. The village is the location of the Shiva Kadappattoor Mahadeva Temple (also Kadappatoor Temple or "Katappattur Temple" or "Kadapattur Mahadeva Temple") Thousands of pilgrims visit the Kadappattoor temple every year, on their way to Sabarimala.

topPlaces.name: Pala, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Pala (IPA: [paːlaː]) is a municipality in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. It is located 28 km (17.4 mi) north of the district headquarters in Kottayam and about 167 km (103.8 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Panthathala
topPlaces.description: Panthathala is a village in mutholy panchayath, close to Palai in the Kottayam district of Kerala state in India. The river Meenachil flows by on one side of the village.

topPlaces.name: Meenachil
topPlaces.description: Meenachil is the north-eastern region of Kottayam district in Kerala, south India. The name originates from Meenakshi, the Hindu Goddess.

topPlaces.name: Lalam, Kottayam
topPlaces.description: Lalam is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mutholy
topPlaces.description: Mutholy is a village in Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the west side of the City of Pala. Mutholy Gramapanchayath lies on both sides of the Meenachil River.

topPlaces.name: Vallichira
topPlaces.description: Vallichira is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kizhaparayar
topPlaces.description: Kizhaparayar is a small village in Kerala state, India. It is part of the Meenachil Panchayat in Kottayam district.

The village is the location of the Shiva Kadappattoor Mahadeva Temple (also Kadappatoor Temple or "Katappattur Temple" or "Kadapattur Mahadeva Temple") Thousands of pilgrims visit the Kadappattoor temple every year, on their way to Sabarimala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (52 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala?
faq.a: Stays in St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Thomas Cathedral, Pala?
faq.a: The top attractions are Katappattor, Pala, Kerala, Panthathala, Meenachil, Lalam, Kottayam.
